description: Add missing unit tests for CLI command modules: config command (levenshtein, path resolution, validation), audit command (duration parsing, timestamp formatting), and logs command (tailFile reverse-seek algorithm).

userStories.title: Add unit tests for CLI config command pure functions
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the pure utility functions in the config command so that input validation, fuzzy matching, and path resolution are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover levenshtein: identical strings return 0, single edit distance, completely different strings, empty strings, case sensitivity
Tests cover suggestKey: suggests closest known key for typos, returns null when no close match exists, handles partial prefix matches
Tests cover maskSecret: secrets longer than 8 chars show first4...last4, short secrets show ****
Tests cover resolveStoredPluginPath: handles absolute paths, ~/ paths expand to homedir, relative paths resolve from configDir
userStories.notes: The functions are in platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts. levenshtein (line 353) is a standard edit distance implementation. suggestKey (line 370) uses levenshtein to find the closest known key. maskSecret (line 32) is a simple string truncation. resolveStoredPluginPath (line 293) handles ~/ expansion and relative path resolution. These are all currently unexported — export them for testing. See platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.test.ts for the testing pattern in this package (uses vitest, mocks fs and fetch). KNOWN_KEYS (line 368) is used by suggestKey — may need to export it too or test indirectly.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for CLI audit command pure functions
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the pure utility functions in the audit command so that duration parsing, timestamp formatting, and limit validation are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover parseDuration: valid inputs (30s, 5m, 2h, 1d), invalid inputs (abc, 30x, empty, negative), returns milliseconds
Tests cover formatTimestamp: same-day shows time only (HH:MM:SS), different day shows MM-DD HH:MM:SS, handles midnight edge case
Tests cover formatDuration: sub-second shows ms, 1+ second shows seconds with 1 decimal
Tests cover parseLimit: valid positive integers, rejects 0, rejects negatives, rejects non-integers, rejects non-numeric strings
userStories.notes: The functions are in platform/cli/src/commands/audit.ts. parseDuration (line 53) uses regex to parse duration strings. formatTimestamp (line 62) has same-day vs different-day logic. formatDuration (line 85) is straightforward ms-to-string conversion. parseLimit (line 38) validates positive integers. These are currently unexported — export them for testing. For formatTimestamp tests, use vi.useFakeTimers() to control 'now' for reliable same-day/different-day assertions. See platform/cli/src/commands/status.test.ts for similar pure function testing patterns.

userStories.title: Expand plugin command test coverage
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the untested helper functions in the plugin command so that package name resolution and plugin info reading are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover resolvePackageName: adds opentabs-plugin- prefix for bare names, preserves scoped packages, preserves full opentabs-plugin- names
Tests cover warnIfNotPlugin: warns for packages without opentabs keyword, passes silently for valid plugins
Tests cover removeFromLocalPlugins: removes matching path, handles missing path gracefully, normalizes paths before comparison
Test file is platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.test.ts (extend existing file)
userStories.notes: The functions are in platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts. The existing test file platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.test.ts only tests buildDirectLookupCandidates. Add new describe blocks for resolvePackageName, warnIfNotPlugin, and removeFromLocalPlugins. These functions may need to be exported. removeFromLocalPlugins involves filesystem mocking (reads/writes config.json). Use the existing mock patterns from the file. Check line numbers by searching for 'const resolvePackageName', 'const warnIfNotPlugin', 'const removeFromLocalPlugins' in the source.
